name: LÖVE
description: Free, open-source 2D Lua game framework for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Minimal API with graphics, sound, and physics bindings—favored for game jams and indie projects.

seo.title: LÖVE — Game Development Tools
seo.meta_description: LÖVE: Free, open-source 2D Lua game framework for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Minimal API with graphics, sound, and physics bindings—favored for game jams and in

when_to_use: 2D game jam entry built with Lua in a weekend
Prototype physics sandbox exploring game mechanic ideas
Cross-platform desktop game shipped on itch.io via LÖVE
Minimal game framework chosen for teaching Lua to beginners
Rapid iteration on core loop before porting to larger engine

pros: Minimal API — you can learn it in an afternoon
Perfect for game jams and rapid prototypes
Distributed as tiny binaries for Windows, macOS, Linux
zlib license lets you ship commercial games freely
cons: No built-in editor — code everything by hand
Mobile and console export requires third-party ports
No asset pipeline or visual scene editor
Small library ecosystem compared to Love2Ds heyday
review.verdict: The best hands-on Lua sandbox for jams and learning; ship-quality mobile and console games need more scaffolding.
review.best_for: Solo developers and learners who want Lua, code-first, desktop-only workflows
review.not_for: Teams needing editors, mobile-first targets, or console publishing
